data.title: As a non-engineer user, I receive a numbered explanation before plugin installation, can acknowledge or cancel, and the installer handles idempotency safely
data.description:
Done Definition:
- Before the plugin installation step, installer prints a numbered explanation: (1) what the DSO plugin is, (2) what permission it requires, (3) what will happen next
- Installer pauses for user acknowledgment via read; uses exec < /dev/tty to re-attach stdin when invoked via pipe (see Considerations for safe invocation form)
- EOF or Ctrl-C at the acknowledgment prompt exits with code 1 and prints 'Installation cancelled.'
- Running installer in a fully initialized directory (.dso-init-complete present) exits with informative message and code 0
- Running installer in a partially initialized directory (project dir exists but .dso-init-complete absent) prompts to resume or start fresh; resume continues from last completed step; start fresh removes the partial directory and starts over
- If S1 spike confirmed settings.json failure path: installer explicitly calls 'claude plugin install <url>' after the user acknowledgment pause, before opening claude
- claude launched with exec claude (replaces installer process) so the user lands in Claude Code interactively
- All T1 tests covering S5 behavior pass (GREEN)

Considerations:
- curl | bash pipes stdin to bash, so read calls inside the script read from the pipe (empty/EOF), not the terminal. Canonical workaround: exec < /dev/tty before any read. HOWEVER: if bash is reading the script from stdin (curl | bash), exec < /dev/tty replaces the script-feed stream, which on some bash versions truncates the remainder of the script mid-execution. SAFER INVOCATION: bash <(curl -sL url) — process substitution means bash reads the script from a file descriptor, not stdin, so exec < /dev/tty works correctly and safely. The SC7 curl example MUST use bash <(curl -sL url) as the canonical form.

Escalation Policy: Escalate when blocked
